title: Standalone MCP server binary: `reify mcp-server` CLI subcommand
analysis:
Task 137 adds a `reify mcp-server [file.ri]` CLI subcommand that creates an Engine, loads a .ri file, and runs the MCP server on stdio. The infrastructure is already in place: reify-mcp provides McpServer with dual-mode transport (in-process + stdio via run_stdio()), a ReifyToolContext trait with 15 methods, and 16 registered tools. reify-cli uses manual argv parsing with 4 existing commands (check, build, lsp, gui). The cmd_lsp() pattern shows how to create a tokio runtime for async stdio servers.

The key new work is: (1) a CliToolContext struct implementing ReifyToolContext backed by a real Engine with interior mutability via Mutex<CliState>, (2) file/source management tracking open files and content, (3) wiring everything into a cmd_mcp_server function that follows the cmd_lsp() pattern.

CliToolContext needs to: parse .ri files via reify_syntax::parse, compile via reify_compiler::compile, evaluate via Engine::eval/check, and expose snapshot state (value cells from snapshot.graph.value_cells + snapshot.values, constraints via check, diagnostics from CompiledModule + Engine) through the MCP trait methods.

Navigation/selection tools are no-ops in CLI mode since there's no GUI viewport. Source location lookups use ValueCellDecl.span (byte offsets) converted to line/column via the source string.

The bracket.ri fixture (5 params: width/height/thickness/fillet_radius/hole_diameter, 1 let: volume, 3 constraints) provides a good integration test target. Values are stored in SI (meters), so 80mm = 0.08 internally.

reify-mcp is already a workspace dependency but needs to be added to reify-cli's Cargo.toml. serde_json is already a dev-dependency for tests.

prerequisites.description: Add reify-mcp.workspace = true to [dependencies] in crates/reify-cli/Cargo.toml. Also add serde_json.workspace = true to [dependencies] (needed in mcp_context for JSON value construction in export). Add tokio dev-dependency with test-util feature for integration tests.

steps.description: Write failing test in cli_smoke.rs: add assertion that the help text (stderr from no-args invocation) includes 'mcp-server'. This extends the existing no_args_shows_help_with_all_commands test.

Add 'mcp-server' to help text in main.rs (eprintln line: '  mcp-server [file] [--project-dir <dir>]  Start MCP server (stdin/stdout)'). Add match arm 'mcp-server' => cmd_mcp_server(&args[2..]). Create stub cmd_mcp_server function that prints usage and returns FAILURE. Add 'mod mcp_context;' to main.rs. Create empty mcp_context.rs file with a placeholder comment.

Write failing integration test in new file mcp_integration.rs: spawn 'reify mcp-server tests/fixtures/bracket.ri', write JSON-RPC initialize request then tools/list request to stdin (newline-delimited), read response lines from stdout, parse as JSON, verify tools/list response has result.tools array with exactly 16 entries. Use std::process::Command with piped stdin/stdout and a timeout via std::thread::spawn + wait_with_output.

Create CliToolContext in mcp_context.rs: struct wrapping Mutex<CliState> where CliState holds Engine, Option<CompiledModule>, HashMap<String, FileEntry> for open files, Option<String> for active file path, PathBuf for project_dir. FileEntry has content: String and dirty: bool. Implement all 15 ReifyToolContext methods with minimal stubs: read methods return empty vecs/default values, get_source returns active file content or ToolError::EngineError, get_selection returns empty SelectionInfo, write methods return ToolError::NotImplemented, navigation returns Ok(false). Add CliToolContext::new(project_dir: PathBuf) constructor and CliToolContext::load_file(&self, path: &str) method that reads from disk, stores in files map, parses+compiles+evals. Implement cmd_mcp_server in main.rs: parse args for optional file.ri and --project-dir flag, create CliToolContext, optionally call load_file, create McpServer::new(Arc::new(context)), run_stdio() via tokio runtime (following cmd_lsp pattern).

Write failing integration test: spawn mcp-server with bracket.ri, send initialize + tools/call request for reify_language_reference with arguments {"topic": "geometry"}, verify response has isError=false and content[0].text contains geometry-related keywords (e.g., 'box', 'cylinder', or 'geometry').

Verify language_reference test passes (the tool handler is self-contained with embedded markdown chunks in tools/reference.rs and tools/language_chunks.rs, independent of context state). Fix if needed -- should pass with the stub context from step-4.

Write failing integration test: spawn mcp-server with bracket.ri, send initialize + tools/call request for reify_get_parameters with empty arguments {}, verify response has isError=false and the returned parameters include entries with names 'width', 'height', 'thickness', 'fillet_radius', 'hole_diameter' (the 5 params from bracket.ri). Verify each has a non-empty value and cell_id containing 'Bracket.'.

Implement real engine integration in CliToolContext::load_file: read file, parse via reify_syntax::parse(source, ModulePath::single(name)), compile via reify_compiler::compile, create Engine::new(Box::new(SimpleConstraintChecker), None), call engine.eval(&compiled), store compiled module. Implement get_parameters: lock state, get snapshot from engine.snapshot(), iterate snapshot.graph.value_cells, for each cell look up (value, determinacy) in snapshot.values, build ParameterInfo { cell_id: id.to_string(), name: id.member.clone(), value: value.to_string(), unit: derive from cell.cell_type dimension or empty string, kind: format cell.kind, entity_path: id.entity.clone(), determinacy: format determinacy }. Implement get_source: return content of requested file or active file. Implement get_open_files: map files entries to OpenFileInfo. Implement get_eval_status: return phase='ready' if engine is initialized, 'idle' otherwise. Implement get_diagnostics: collect from compiled module diagnostics, convert SourceSpan byte offsets to line/column using source content line scanning. Implement get_constraints: call engine.check_snapshot or engine.check on the compiled module to get constraint results, map to ConstraintInfo. Implement get_source_location: search compiled module templates for matching entity path, find ValueCellDecl by member name, convert span to line/column.

Write failing integration test: spawn mcp-server with bracket.ri, send initialize + tools/call request for reify_set_parameter with arguments {"cell_id": "Bracket.width", "value": "100"}, verify response has isError=false and result contains success=true with a new_value field. Then send a subsequent reify_get_parameters call and verify the width parameter's value has changed from its original.

Implement set_parameter in CliToolContext: lock state, parse cell_id string by splitting on '.' into entity+member to create ValueCellId, parse value string as f64, look up the cell's dimension from snapshot.graph.value_cells to determine if it's a length/angle/dimensionless value, construct appropriate Value (Value::Scalar for dimensional, Value::Real for dimensionless, Value::Int if integer), call engine.set_param_and_invalidate(&cell_id, value), then call engine.edit_param(&compiled, &cell_id) to incrementally re-evaluate, return SetParamResult { success: true, new_value: new_value.to_string(), unit }. Implement update_source: replace file content in files map, set dirty=true, re-run full parse→compile→eval pipeline (topology may change), return UpdateResult with diagnostics count. Implement open_file: read from disk, add to files map, set as active, parse+compile+eval if .ri file. Implement save_file: write file content to disk, set dirty=false. Implement export: call engine.build(&compiled, format) with appropriate ExportFormat, write geometry_output to path.

design_decisions.decision: Use Mutex<CliState> for interior mutability in CliToolContext
design_decisions.rationale: ReifyToolContext trait methods take &self (object-safe, Arc-compatible), but Engine::eval/set_param_and_invalidate need &mut self. Wrapping state in Mutex allows safe interior mutation. Since the MCP server processes one request at a time (newline-delimited JSON-RPC on stdio, sequential dispatch in run_on_streams), contention is not a concern.

design_decisions.decision: CliToolContext goes in reify-cli, not reify-mcp
design_decisions.rationale: It depends on Engine, compiler, syntax parser, SimpleConstraintChecker — heavy dependencies that should not leak into the protocol-only reify-mcp crate. reify-mcp stays transport/protocol focused with ReifyToolContext as the clean abstraction boundary.

design_decisions.decision: Navigation tools (focus_entity, navigate_to_source) return Ok(false) in CLI mode
design_decisions.rationale: Task description says 'return success but note that GUI is not available (no-op in CLI mode)'. Returning Ok(false) signals the operation was recognized but could not be performed. Selection returns empty SelectionInfo (no GUI viewport).

design_decisions.decision: Re-eval from scratch on update_source rather than incremental
design_decisions.rationale: Source changes may alter the topology (add/remove params, constraints), invalidating the entire evaluation graph. A cold restart (parse -> compile -> eval) is the correct approach. Incremental eval via set_param_and_invalidate + edit_param is only valid for parameter value changes within the same topology.

design_decisions.decision: Integration tests use std::process::Command with piped stdin/stdout
design_decisions.rationale: Matches the existing CLI smoke test pattern in cli_smoke.rs using env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_reify"). Spawning the actual binary tests the full end-to-end flow including arg parsing, engine initialization, and MCP transport. Tests write newline-delimited JSON to stdin, close stdin, then read all stdout.

design_decisions.decision: Parse set_parameter value as f64 and infer dimension from the cell's existing type
design_decisions.rationale: The MCP protocol passes values as strings. For scalar parameters, parsing as f64 and applying the cell's known dimension (from the snapshot's ValueCellNode.cell_type) gives the correct Value::Scalar. This matches how the GUI sets parameter values — the dimension is intrinsic to the cell definition, so '100' for a length param means 100 in the cell's SI dimension.

design_decisions.decision: Initialize Engine without geometry kernel (None) by default
design_decisions.rationale: Geometry kernel (OcctKernelHandle::spawn()) is only needed for export/build operations. Initializing without it keeps startup fast and avoids OCCT dependency issues. The kernel can be lazily initialized when export is first called, or the export method can create a temporary DispatchPlanner.
Task title specifies 'mcp-server' explicitly. This is descriptive and follows the pattern of compound CLI subcommands. The match arm uses the string literal directly.

reuse.how: Create McpServer::new(Arc::new(context)) and call server.run_stdio(). All 16 tools, JSON-RPC dispatch via McpDispatcher, and stdio transport with newline-delimited JSON are already implemented.

CliToolContext implements the 15-method ReifyToolContext trait, returning types like ParameterInfo, ConstraintInfo, DiagnosticInfo, EvalStatusInfo, etc. from the engine state. Import via reify_mcp::{ReifyToolContext, ParameterInfo, ...}.
Reuse Engine::new(Box::new(SimpleConstraintChecker), None) pattern for headless MCP mode. Add geometry kernel only if export is needed.

Reference the pattern of reading file -> reify_syntax::parse(&source, ModulePath::single(name)) -> reify_compiler::compile(&parsed) for CliToolContext::load_file. The pattern handles error reporting for parse/compile failures.

reuse.what: cmd_lsp pattern for tokio runtime + stdio server
reuse.how: Follow identical pattern: tokio::runtime::Runtime::new() -> rt.block_on(server.run_stdio()) -> ExitCode::SUCCESS. Error handling on runtime creation failure.

reuse.what: CLI smoke test pattern with env!(CARGO_BIN_EXE_reify)
reuse.how: Extend existing help text test for mcp-server. Use Command::new(env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_reify")) with piped stdin/stdout for integration tests.
Reference JSON-RPC request format ({"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":N,"method":"tools/list","params":{}}), response parsing, and assertion patterns for tool count, tool names, and error checking.

Use as standard test input for MCP integration tests. Contains structure Bracket with 5 params (width=80mm, height=100mm, thickness=5mm, fillet_radius=3mm, hole_diameter=6mm), 1 let (volume), 3 constraints, and 1 realization (box).
ValueCellId displays as 'Entity.member' (e.g., 'Bracket.width'). Parse back by splitting on '.' for set_parameter. Use ValueCellId::new(entity, member) constructor.
